data.epi_content:
As I sat drinking tea for quite some time, I suddenly thought of the gun and walked to the drawer to retrieve the pistol and plenty of bullets.

"How sad it was when this was in that person's hands."

I began inserting bullets one by one into the empty magazine.

Then, as I always did, I started to wear it at my waist.

So no one could see it.

Tap. Tap.

I set the remaining bullets upright on the table and was blankly looking around the room when someone knocked on the door.

Since there was only one person who would visit me, I hurried to the entrance and opened the door.

The sensation of opening a door with my own hands after so long was a kind of emotional experience.

Despite having perfectly functional arms and legs, I hadn't been able to move freely, being confined somewhere, and even when I approached a door to open it, it never opened except for once.

And even that one time was deliberately left open to make me despair when I came out and found people blocking my way...

It's not exactly a pleasant story.

I've returned now, and it would be rude to remain downcast in front of the person who saved me.

Yes, that would be rude. He said he would come visit me.

After opening the door, my body stiffened slightly.

Not because of any negative emotions.

Just because Evan was standing right in front of me.

While I was imprisoned, I thought I was torturing myself with false hope by thinking Evan would save me.

But here he is, the Evan who really did save me, who pulled me out of that hell.

The one who said he would prevent me from experiencing misfortune, protect me from terrible things, and keep me safe.

Come to think of it, those would be incredibly embarrassing words to hear while sober—I wonder if my face turned red when I heard them.

At that time, my eyes had been gouged out, so I couldn't even remember what expression Evan had while looking at me.

Hahaha, I never knew that being able to see properly could be such a joyful thing.

"Hello."

With a slightly emotional feeling, I lifted the corners of my mouth and greeted him.

And when I greeted him, he slightly raised the corners of his mouth too, and replied in a rather calm tone, unlike the Evan I used to know.

"Yes, hello."

After exchanging such light greetings rather than any dramatic emotional expressions, I let Evan into the entrance.

"Why don't you come in and have a cup of tea?"

Instead of answering, Evan nodded.

The slightly sorrowful and anger-filled voice I had heard back then was nowhere to be found, and he simply sat down calmly in the room.

After I had brewed tea from the shelf where I kept small amounts of tea, coffee, and chocolate, and was about to bring it to Evan, I saw him expressionlessly picking up one of the remaining bullets with his finger and examining it from different angles.

"Why did you take out the bullets?"

"There's someone I want to shoot."

A moment of silence followed that statement.

As I approached and placed a cup of tea in front of him, putting chocolate on the table, Evan asked with an expression that suggested this was somewhat difficult to say.

"That person isn't me or you... is it?"

"Fortunately not."

Then I took a sip of tea.

After enjoying the aroma of the tea once, I savored the taste of the hard chocolate in my mouth and then drank the hot tea again, enjoying food that actually tasted like human food after so long. While doing so, I noticed Evan tilting his head.

"Is something uncomfortable?"

"It's just different from what I expected."

I roughly understood what he meant, but I pretended not to and asked a question.
data.epi_content2:
"I'm not sure what's different from what you expected."

At that, Evan hesitated slightly before beginning to share his thoughts.

His speaking voice was quite warm, pleasant to listen to, unlike the sharp voice from when he had promised to save me.

"I expected a dramatic reunion.

As soon as you saw my face, tears would well up in your eyes, and you'd hug me and cry.

And I would hold you and comfort you."

"...That sounds nice."

"Right?"

"I'm hungry, so let's have tea and chocolate first, whether we're talking or having an emotional reunion.

I was waiting because it felt a bit awkward to eat alone.

It's been almost half a year since I've had proper food, so we should celebrate."

"...Ah."

Evan made a stunned expression, as if he hadn't thought of that.

"That's right."

We drank tea and ate chocolate in silence.

There was a somewhat awkward atmosphere, but it wasn't uncomfortable.

"You seem quite composed after everything you've been through."

"I have to be composed, this is all I have.

A bit of dignity and common sense, and a few bullets.

And stubbornness and obstinacy that others might find useless.

Thanks to that, I was quite disobedient even when I was taken away."

Evan frowned slightly before smoothing his expression again.

I silently enjoyed the sweetness and distinctive aroma of the chocolate.

The sugar melting in my mouth was enough to make me forget about the tube that had been stuck in my throat, forcing feed into my stomach.

After about ten minutes had passed, Evan put down his tea and said quietly.

"Erica, would it be alright if I gave you just one hug?"

Hearing those words, I stood up without answering and spread my arms in acceptance.

Then, Evan approached and embraced me tightly.

While the feeling wasn't particularly bad, I couldn't help but feel a bit depressed as I listened to him sobbing slightly and repeatedly whispering "I'm sorry" in my ear.

There's no need for him to feel guilty.

If he hadn't come to save me, I would have completely lost my mind and become like a rocking doll that couldn't move properly, following whatever the Crown Prince ordered like that nameless woman in the Imperial Palace.

And perhaps that would have been something that could hardly be called me, more like a robot that moved according to commands...

"I'm sorry."

As Evan continued to murmur quietly, I asked him a question.

"Are you sorry to me?"

"...Yes."

"You don't need to be. Until now, there wasn't a single person who looked out for me, but now I have at least one."

There were no sobs, sniffles, emotional tears, or tears of joy.

We were both too dried up for that.

Both me and him—living with a blade turned against the Emperor must have been incredibly difficult, and asking how hard it was or saying "you've been through a lot" would only be mockery to each other.

Both Evan and I knew that very well.

Nevertheless, even if we didn't cry sadly, it would be okay to shed at least one small tear.

I let a slightly salty tear fall and run down my cheek, then wiped it against his shoulder.
data.epi_content3:
Had my emotions become a bit intense?

My feet began to lift off the floor.

It was clearly a hug at first, but being lifted while embraced—this isn't a hug anymore.

I'm not sure what to call it.

Feeling slightly embarrassed, I threw out a half-joking, half-serious comment to end the hug and separate.

"Why, wasn't today... what was it again?

Wasn't it the day you came looking for me because I slapped Vivian's cheek?

Are you perhaps hugging me so tightly out of anger that my body is being crushed?

My feet aren't touching the floor."

At those words, Evan's body flinched slightly.

Then he gently lowered me down.

Perhaps because I had been held so tightly, I felt slightly dizzy when my feet touched the ground, and I staggered before collapsing onto the bed.

"Heh, I'm just kidding."

At that, Evan made a slightly sulky expression.

It feels like I can finally breathe a little.

My goodness, that Evan.

The same Evan who used to tear me apart with his words and wouldn't even give me a glance, who thought I was some wild animal that would recover if only he kept me locked up when I showed signs of breaking—how did that foolish gentleman change like this?

Since he said he went back in time, since he knew I would go back, since he survived opposing the Emperor and saved me, he must have gone through a lot.

My curiosity is making me itch to ask, but there's plenty of time. There's no need to ask about such things now.

How could I tell him that while he was running around killing people to save me, I was locked in a soft room, drinking water through my nostrils, being humiliated by Blacks and raised like livestock?

It's good that he saved me, but I hope he won't lock me up like he did with Vivian.

I hope he won't dismiss whatever I say as the ravings of a madwoman and ignore me.

Even now, just thinking about that time makes me feel a bit fearful.

"Evan."

I spoke to Evan while looking at the ceiling.

"Now listen to what I have to say too."

"Alright."

"Don't leave me alone."

When I said that, Evan trembled slightly.

Then, with a slightly warm smile—the kind he used to give Vivian, which brings back memories of how jealous I felt—he answered.

"Until when?"

"Until I die."

When I said that, Evan retorted as if it were some terrible joke.

But how sad I had been, thinking that I alone was the only human being alive and moving in this clock, and that the rest were puppets created to torment me.

"I've chased after you through death so many times, so that much is nothing."

"I'm touched."

For a meeting between a person who had been saved and the one who had saved them, the atmosphere was quite calm.

So, to show my respect, I got up from the bed and approached Evan.

Then, pretending to kneel on one knee, I bowed and lightly pressed my lips to the back of his right hand.

He didn't pull his hand away or show any panic.

I kept my lips pressed against his hand for quite some time, feeling the warmth of his skin.

For a lonely person without anyone around, the warmth of human contact after so long was far more moving than eating chocolate after a long time.

Amusingly so.
